authors.name: Octavia Butler
authors.bio: American science fiction author known for blending African American spirituality with science fiction.

authors.name: Toni Morrison
authors.bio: Nobel Prize-winning novelist celebrated for her powerful exploration of Black identity and American history.

authors.name: N.K. Jemisin
authors.bio: First author to win the Hugo Award for Best Novel three years in a row for the Broken Earth trilogy.

authors.name: James Baldwin
authors.bio: Essayist and novelist whose works explored racial and social issues in America.

books.title: Kindred
books.description: A modern Black woman is transported back in time to the antebellum South.

books.title: Parable of the Sower
books.description: In a dystopian future California, a young woman with hyperempathy syndrome founds a new faith.

books.title: Beloved
books.description: A formerly enslaved woman is haunted by the ghost of her deceased daughter.

books.title: Song of Solomon
books.description: A young African American man discovers his family history and identity through a mythical journey.

books.title: The Fifth Season
books.description: On a continent plagued by catastrophic seismic events, a woman searches for her kidnapped daughter.

books.title: The City We Became
books.description: New York City comes alive as its boroughs are embodied by human avatars who must defend it.

books.title: Go Tell It on the Mountain
books.description: A semi-autobiographical novel about a young boy's spiritual awakening in 1930s Harlem.

books.title: Giovanni's Room
books.description: An American man in Paris grapples with his identity and a love affair with an Italian bartender.
